title: Rivian Q1 Earnings: R2 Ramp and Liquidity Update
text: Rivian delivered earnings largely aligned with expectations. The company reported Q1 revenue of $1.38 billion versus $1.39 billion consensus, up 11% year-over-year. Net loss per share came in at $0.33, significantly better than the anticipated $0.72 loss. Gross profit reached $119 million, marking the third consecutive profitable quarter on that metric. However, adjusted EBITDA swung to a loss of $472 million. The highly anticipated R2 midsize SUV is entering production phase. CEO RJ Scaringe stated the company is now ramping production, starting deliveries beyond employees, to customers. Production ramp will concentrate in Q3 and Q4 of 2026, with customer deliveries beginning later this spring. The company maintains its full-year delivery guidance of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles. Rivian ended Q1 with $4.83 billion in cash and equivalents, down from $6.08 billion at year-end 2025. Total accessible liquidity stands at $13.6 billion when including committed funding from Volkswagen, Uber, and the revised DOE loan. CEO Scaringe noted: With the increase in volume, you have more fixed cost absorption, so the cost of goods sold will come down meaningfully. The company maintained its 2026 adjusted EBITDA loss guidance of $1.80-$2.10 billion.
